reasoning: The base rate for any album spending 16 consecutive weeks at #1 on the Billboard 200 is extremely low (basically 0% in the modern streaming era; the last to do so was the Titanic soundtrack in 1998). While 'The Life of a Showgirl' had a massive record-breaking debut of over 4 million units, it suffered a massive 92% decay in week 2, landing at 338,000 units. To reach 16 straight weeks, it would have to maintain the #1 position from its October release all the way through Late November and December. Historically, the holiday season sees legacy Christmas albums (e.g., Michael Bublé, Mariah Carey, Brenda Lee, etc.) surge to take the top spots on both the Billboard Hot 100 and Billboard 200, which would definitively break any consecutive streak. Considering the steep second-week drop and the formidable obstacle of holiday charting trends, a 16-week straight run is virtually impossible. I cap my prediction at 0.05 following the requested bounds.
batch_queries: Will 'The Life of a Showgirl' be #1 for 16 straight weeks?
